steps.title: Identify what the student wants to emphasize
steps.content:
The student wants to highlight fluorite's hardness relative to talc and quartz using the Mohs scale. The Mohs rule: a higher-numbered mineral scratches a lower-numbered one. Fluorite = 4, talc = 1, quartz = 7. The correct answer must use fluorite as the subject and compare it to both talc and quartz.
  • "Quartz is hard enough to scratch both talc and fluorite.": Centers on quartz, not fluorite; fluorite's hardness relative to talc is never addressed. ✗
  • "Fluorite is hard enough to scratch talc but not hard enough to scratch quartz.": Makes fluorite the subject, correctly shows it outranks talc (4 > 1) but falls below quartz (4 < 7), directly emphasizing fluorite's position between the two. ✓
  • "In the Mohs scale of mineral hardness, quartz (7) is ranked higher than talc (1).": Compares only quartz and talc; fluorite is not mentioned at all. ✗
  • "Quartz, talc, and fluorite can be ordered by hardness based on their ability to scratch other minerals.": A vague general statement that does not specify fluorite's actual position or hardness relative to either mineral. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Fluorite is hard enough to scratch talc but not hard enough to scratch quartz. is the only option that centers on fluorite and explicitly shows its relationship to both talc and quartz using the Mohs logic. That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
